problem_statement: The document examines how LLMs automate code review and deployment in CI/CD pipelines, and argues that AI-driven CI introduces security vulnerabilities, model bias, and errors that still require human supervision to catch.
magnitude_evidence: AI-based static analysis tools find 50% more security issues than conventional approaches but lack contextual awareness; 60% of survey respondents raised false-positive/security-risk concerns
insufficiency_evidence: AI-powered static analysis tools miss subtle vulnerabilities and come with false positives; AI suggestions are not project-specific and require human intervention; overreliance on AI-driven testing results in omission of edge cases only human testers can identify
solution_mechanisms: combine AI static analysis with human review
automated cross-validation across multiple AI models
fairness boundaries and bias audits of training data
notes: Text is garbled/machine-extracted in places with grammatical errors; some cited statistics (30%/40%/50%) are loosely attributed to unnamed literature.
